title: erigeron borealis var. alpiniformis
intro: Erigeron borealis var. alpiniformis, also known as alpine fleabane, is a plant species belonging to the sunflower family, native to high-altitude mountainous regions. Traditionally, alpine fleabane has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues and sore throats, as well as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Alpine fleabane can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with the leaves and flowers typically used in herbal remedies to harness its medicinal properties.
